content.code: std::cout << "b is a " << b << '\n'; // much better

list: It makes the assumption that the desired output can be represented as a single std::string.Ouridentify()member function does not have access to the stream object.
Ouridentify()member function does not have access to the stream object.

content.text:
This outputs:Base
Derived: Employee(Jim, 4)
Derived: Employee(Jim, 4)In this version,Base::operator<<doesn’t do any printing itself. Instead, it just calls virtual member functionprint()and passes it the stream object. Theprint()function then uses this stream object to do its own printing.Base::print()uses the stream object to print “Base”. More interestingly,Derived::print()uses the stream object to print both “Derived: ” and to callEmployee::operator<<to print the value of memberm_e. The latter would have been more challenging to do in the prior example!Next lesson25.xChapter 25 summary and quizBack to table of contentsPrevious lesson25.10Dynamic casting
